peripherals.label: Quectel BG770A Cellular
peripherals.summary: LTE-M / NB-IoT cellular modem with the full Quectel AT command surface.
peripherals.detail: Byte-exact V.250 + Quectel +QI*/+QMT*/+QHTTP*/+QGPS*/+QSSL* state machines, validated against real BG770A-GL hardware captures. Firmware sends AT commands, modem replies stream back over UART.

config_keys.name: apn
config_keys.doc: APN to set on the PDP context (e.g. "internet").

config_keys.name: rssi
config_keys.doc: Initial signal strength reported by AT+CSQ (0..99).

config_keys.name: ber
config_keys.doc: Initial bit-error-rate reported by AT+CSQ (0..99, defaults to 99).

config_keys.name: boot_urcs
config_keys.doc: If true, the modem emits the cold-boot URC sequence on attach.

config_keys.name: auto_attach
config_keys.doc: If true, the modem reports itself already registered + attached at boot.

peripherals.label: NEO-6M GPS
peripherals.summary: GPS module streaming NMEA sentences over UART RX.
peripherals.detail: GGA + RMC sentences with XOR checksum, generated entirely in the Rust core. Firmware echoes the stream back to the host UART.

config_keys.name: lat_deg
config_keys.doc: Initial latitude in decimal degrees (paired with lon_deg).

config_keys.name: lon_deg
config_keys.doc: Initial longitude in decimal degrees (paired with lat_deg).

peripherals.label: ADXL345 Tilt
peripherals.summary: 3-axis ±2/4/8/16 g digital accelerometer over I2C.
peripherals.detail: Analog Devices ADXL345 with the canonical 0x53 / 0x1D 7-bit address pair. Wired through the simulated I2C bus; host stimulus seeds X/Y/Z and the firmware reads them through the DATAX/Y/Z registers.

config_keys.name: i2c_address
config_keys.doc: 7-bit slave address. Defaults to 0x53; 0x1D selects the alternate-pin variant.

peripherals.label: INA219 Power Monitor
peripherals.summary: High-side current and bus voltage sensor over I²C.
peripherals.detail: Texas Instruments INA219 (default 7-bit address 0x40). Host stimulus sets bus voltage and load current; firmware reads config/shunt/bus/current/power/cal registers on the simulated I²C bus.

config_keys.name: i2c_address
config_keys.doc: 7-bit slave address. Defaults to 0x40 (A0/A1 ground).

peripherals.label: ADS1115 16-bit ADC
peripherals.summary: 4-channel 16-bit I²C ADC (TI ADS1115).
peripherals.detail: Texas Instruments ADS1115 (default address 0x48). Host stimulus sets A0–A3 voltages; firmware reads conversion/config over I²C with the standard pointer protocol used by Arduino ADS1X15 drivers.

config_keys.name: i2c_address
config_keys.doc: 7-bit slave address. Defaults to 0x48 (ADDR→GND).

peripherals.label: DS3231 RTC
peripherals.summary: I²C real-time clock with temperature-compensated crystal.
peripherals.detail: Maxim DS3231 (address 0x68). BCD time registers 0x00–0x06 and control/status for common Arduino RTC libraries. Host can set unix_time or temperature via SimInput.

config_keys.name: i2c_address
config_keys.doc: 7-bit slave address. Defaults to 0x68.

peripherals.label: HX711 Load Cell
peripherals.summary: 24-bit load-cell amplifier (DT/SCK bit-bang).
peripherals.detail: Avia HX711. Firmware bit-bangs SCK while reading DT; host sets weight (g) or raw counts via SimInput. Gain/channel selection pulses after bit 24 are accepted.

config_keys.name: sck_pin
config_keys.doc: SCK GPIO pin (e.g. "PA8"). Defaults to PA8.

config_keys.name: dt_pin
config_keys.doc: DT (DOUT) GPIO pin (e.g. "PA9"). Defaults to PA9.

peripherals.label: AS5600 Magnetic Encoder
peripherals.summary: 12-bit contactless magnetic rotary position sensor over I²C.
peripherals.detail: ams AS5600 (default address 0x36). Host sets angle in degrees; firmware reads RAW_ANGLE / ANGLE (12-bit) and STATUS magnet-detected bit.

config_keys.name: i2c_address
config_keys.doc: 7-bit slave address. Defaults to 0x36.

peripherals.label: VL53L0X ToF
peripherals.summary: Laser time-of-flight distance sensor over I²C (ST VL53L0X).
peripherals.detail: STMicroelectronics VL53L0X at 0x29. Identification + range result registers for common Arduino drivers; host sets distance in mm via SimInput.

config_keys.name: i2c_address
config_keys.doc: 7-bit slave address. Defaults to 0x29.

peripherals.label: BNO055 IMU
peripherals.summary: 9-DoF absolute orientation sensor (Bosch BNO055) over I²C.
peripherals.detail: CHIP_ID 0xA0, operation mode, and Euler heading/roll/pitch registers for Adafruit BNO055 sketches. Host sets euler angles via SimInput; fusion is not simulated.

config_keys.name: i2c_address
config_keys.doc: 7-bit slave address. Defaults to 0x28 (COM3 low).

peripherals.label: HC-05 Bluetooth
peripherals.summary: Bluetooth SPP module with AT command shell over UART.
peripherals.detail: Thin AT echo model (OK to AT/AT+…). No RF pairing or wireless data path.

peripherals.label: nRF24L01+
peripherals.summary: 2.4 GHz SPI transceiver register shell (no air link).
peripherals.detail: Nordic nRF24L01+ SPI R_REGISTER/W_REGISTER model for CONFIG/STATUS and companion registers. RF packet radio is not simulated.

config_keys.name: cs_pin
config_keys.doc: CSN GPIO pin (e.g. "PA4"). Defaults to PA4.

peripherals.label: microSD (SPI)
peripherals.summary: SPI SD card command shell (init + single-block read zeros).
peripherals.detail: CMD0/8/55/41/58/17 shell for common Arduino card-init paths. No real filesystem; CMD17 returns a 512-byte zero block.

config_keys.name: cs_pin
config_keys.doc: Card CS GPIO pin (e.g. "PA4"). Defaults to PA4.

peripherals.label: MCP2515 CAN
peripherals.summary: SPI CAN controller register shell (no bus frames).
peripherals.detail: Microchip MCP2515 RESET/READ/WRITE/BIT_MODIFY for CANCTRL/CANSTAT and friends. CAN wire protocol is not simulated in this thin shell.

config_keys.name: cs_pin
config_keys.doc: Chip-select GPIO pin (e.g. "PA4"). Defaults to PA4.

peripherals.label: MPU6050 IMU
peripherals.summary: 6-axis gyro + accelerometer over I2C.
peripherals.detail: InvenSense MPU-6050 with WHO_AM_I = 0x68. Reports static sample values today; host stimulus hooks into the WASM bridge for live updates.

config_keys.name: i2c_address
config_keys.doc: 7-bit slave address. Defaults to 0x68; 0x69 selects the AD0=high variant.

peripherals.label: BME280 Weather
peripherals.summary: Bosch BME280 temp + humidity + pressure sensor over I2C.
peripherals.detail: Serves factory calibration coefficients plus raw ADC words, exactly like the silicon; the firmware driver recovers engineering units by running Bosch's compensation formulas. Stimulus takes °C / %RH / hPa and inverts that same compensation against the same coefficients, so a real driver reads back what was set. Defaults to 25 °C / 50 %RH / 1013.25 hPa.

config_keys.name: i2c_address
config_keys.doc: 7-bit slave address. Defaults to 0x76; 0x77 selects the SDO=VDDIO variant.

peripherals.label: SSD1306 OLED
peripherals.summary: 128×64 monochrome OLED display over I2C with a paged framebuffer.
peripherals.detail: Solomon Systech SSD1306 with the canonical 0x3C / 0x3D address pair. Tracks the 8-page-by-128-column framebuffer; the WASM bridge surfaces pixel state for the playground's display overlay.

config_keys.name: i2c_address
config_keys.doc: 7-bit slave address. Defaults to 0x3C; 0x3D selects the SA0=high variant.

peripherals.label: SSD1306 OLED 0.91″
peripherals.summary: 0.91″ 128×32 monochrome OLED display over I2C with a paged framebuffer.
peripherals.detail: Solomon Systech SSD1306 in the 0.91-inch 128×32 form factor (4 GDDRAM pages). Identical command set to the 128×64 panel — only the page count differs. Canonical 0x3C / 0x3D address pair; the WASM bridge surfaces pixel state for the playground's display overlay.

config_keys.name: i2c_address
config_keys.doc: 7-bit slave address. Defaults to 0x3C; 0x3D selects the SA0=high variant.

peripherals.label: SH1107 OLED
peripherals.summary: 128×128 monochrome OLED display over I2C with a paged framebuffer.
peripherals.detail: Sino Wealth SH1107 128×128 OLED (e.g. the 1.5" GME128128-01-IIC module) on the canonical 0x3C / 0x3D address pair. Tracks the 16-page-by-128-column framebuffer; the WASM bridge surfaces pixel state for the playground's display overlay.

config_keys.name: i2c_address
config_keys.doc: 7-bit slave address. Defaults to 0x3C; 0x3D selects the SA0=high variant.

peripherals.label: ILI9341 TFT
peripherals.summary: 240×320 RGB565 SPI TFT display.
peripherals.detail: Implements the cmd / RAMWR SPI protocol against an in-memory framebuffer. The playground surfaces pixels through the simulator bridge so the host can render the display verbatim.

config_keys.name: cs_pin
config_keys.doc: Chip-select GPIO pin (e.g. "PA4"). Defaults to PA4.

peripherals.label: MAX31855 Thermocouple
peripherals.summary: Cold-junction-compensated K-type thermocouple amplifier (read-only SPI).
peripherals.detail: Returns the 32-bit MAX31855 response on every CS-framed transaction. Host stimulus seeds thermocouple + cold-junction temperatures; bit layout matches the real silicon's datasheet exactly.

config_keys.name: cs_pin
config_keys.doc: Chip-select GPIO pin (e.g. "PA4"). Defaults to PA4.

peripherals.label: SSD1680 Tri-Color E-Paper
peripherals.summary: 2.9" tri-color (black/white/red) SSD1680 e-paper over SPI.
peripherals.detail: Full SPI command + display-data RAM state machine, validated by the e2e e-paper integration test. Same model drives both the STM32F103 lab and the ESP32 playground board.

config_keys.name: cs_pin
config_keys.doc: Chip-select GPIO pin (e.g. "PA4"). Defaults to PA4.

peripherals.label: 74HC165 Shift Register
peripherals.summary: 8-bit parallel-in / serial-out shift register over SPI.
peripherals.detail: Lets a host MCU sample 8 GPIO inputs through one SPI clock burst. Used in the IO-Link DI/DO lab to surface field-side switch state.

config_keys.name: cs_pin
config_keys.doc: Chip-select GPIO pin (e.g. "PA4"). Defaults to PA4.

config_keys.name: inputs
config_keys.doc: Optional initial 8-bit input state (0..0xFF) — useful for static test stimulus.

peripherals.label: 74HC595 7-Segment (4-digit)
peripherals.summary: 4-digit 7-segment LED display driven by two chained 74HC595 shift registers over SPI.
peripherals.detail: The classic blue 4-digit module: a segment 74HC595 and a digit-select 74HC595 in series. Firmware shifts a segment byte then a digit-select byte (16 bits), pulses RCLK (SPI chip-select) to latch, and multiplexes the four digits. The model auto-detects the digit-select byte, decodes the standard a–g/dp segment font, and surfaces the four displayed characters.

config_keys.name: cs_pin
config_keys.doc: RCLK latch GPIO pin, wired as SPI chip-select (e.g. "PA4"). Defaults to PA4.

peripherals.label: TM1637 7-Segment (4-digit)
peripherals.summary: 4-digit 7-segment LED display with the TM1637 2-wire (CLK/DIO) driver.
peripherals.detail: The RobotDyn-style 4-digit clock display. The host bit-bangs two GPIO lines with the TM1637's I²C-like, LSB-first, address-less protocol; the model observes both output pins through the bus write-hook, decodes start/stop/data/ACK framing and the data / address / display-control commands, and renders the four grids through the standard a–g/dp segment font (including the clock colon).

config_keys.name: clk_pin
config_keys.doc: CLK GPIO pin the firmware bit-bangs (e.g. "PA8"). Defaults to PA8.

config_keys.name: dio_pin
config_keys.doc: DIO GPIO pin the firmware bit-bangs (e.g. "PA9"). Defaults to PA9.

peripherals.label: 7-Segment Display (single digit)
peripherals.summary: Bare single-digit 7-segment LED display driven straight from nine GPIO pins.
peripherals.detail: The raw display with no driver chip: segment pins A–G plus DP and one common pin, each wired directly to a GPIO. The model samples all nine output registers after every write to a hosting port and recomputes the lit segments combinationally — there is no protocol or state machine. COM polarity is inferred from the level the firmware holds the common pin at: COM low means common cathode (a segment lights when its pin is HIGH), COM high means common anode (a segment lights when its pin is LOW). The readback always reports which segments are lit regardless of wiring, decoded to a character through the standard a–g/dp segment font.

config_keys.name: a_pin
config_keys.doc: GPIO pin driving segment A (e.g. "PA0"). Defaults to PA0.

config_keys.name: b_pin
config_keys.doc: GPIO pin driving segment B (e.g. "PA1"). Defaults to PA1.

config_keys.name: c_pin
config_keys.doc: GPIO pin driving segment C (e.g. "PA2"). Defaults to PA2.

config_keys.name: d_pin
config_keys.doc: GPIO pin driving segment D (e.g. "PA3"). Defaults to PA3.

config_keys.name: e_pin
config_keys.doc: GPIO pin driving segment E (e.g. "PA4"). Defaults to PA4.

config_keys.name: f_pin
config_keys.doc: GPIO pin driving segment F (e.g. "PA5"). Defaults to PA5.

config_keys.name: g_pin
config_keys.doc: GPIO pin driving segment G (e.g. "PA6"). Defaults to PA6.

config_keys.name: dp_pin
config_keys.doc: GPIO pin driving the decimal point (e.g. "PA7"). Defaults to PA7.

config_keys.name: com_pin
config_keys.doc: GPIO pin driving the common pin (e.g. "PA8"). Its level selects the wiring: low = common cathode, high = common anode. Defaults to PA8.

peripherals.label: Nokia 5110 LCD
peripherals.summary: Nokia 5110 (PCD8544) monochrome 84×48 LCD over SPI.
peripherals.detail: Tracks the 504-byte (84×48 / 8) DDRAM, command vs data discrimination via a host GPIO D/C line that the bus resolves to a concrete GPIO ODR address at attach time.

config_keys.name: cs_pin
config_keys.doc: Chip-select GPIO pin (e.g. "PB6"). Defaults to PB6.

config_keys.name: dc_pin
config_keys.doc: Data/command GPIO pin (e.g. "PC7"). Defaults to PC7. Resolved to the driving GPIO's ODR address at attach time.

peripherals.label: IO-Link Master
peripherals.summary: IO-Link master state machine over UART.
peripherals.detail: Drives wake-up / startup / operate cycles, m-sequence types, process-data exchange. The IO-Link DI/DO device demo uses this to host two digital-input channels.

config_keys.name: pd_in_len
config_keys.doc: Process-data input length in bytes. Defaults to 1 (single-byte DI device).

config_keys.name: m_seq_type
config_keys.doc: M-sequence type (1..6). Used to derive od_len: types ≥ 4 use 2-byte OD frames.

config_keys.name: com
config_keys.doc: Communication speed: "COM1" (4.8 kbaud), "COM2" (38.4 kbaud, default), or "COM3" (230.4 kbaud).

config_keys.name: frame_gap_ticks
config_keys.doc: Inter-frame gap in UART ticks (default 6000). A faster -O2 device can use a small gap so many cyclic reads fit the step budget.

peripherals.label: NTC Thermistor
peripherals.summary: 10 kΩ NTC + voltage divider on an ADC channel.
peripherals.detail: Beta-equation thermistor model (R0 = 10 kΩ at 25 °C, B = 3950) feeding a 10 kΩ pull-down divider. Drive the `temperature` channel (°C) at runtime and the ADC channel follows through the real resistance→voltage math. Starts at 25 °C, where the divider sits at exactly Vref/2.

config_keys.name: channel
config_keys.doc: ADC channel index (0..N). Defaults to 0.

peripherals.label: Potentiometer
peripherals.summary: 3-pin linear potentiometer on an ADC channel (rotary or slide).
peripherals.detail: Linear voltage-divider model: the wiper taps off Vref * position/100. Drive the `position` channel (0..100 %) at runtime and the ADC channel follows through the real divider math. Starts centred at 50 %. A rotary pot and a slide pot are electrically identical and share this model.

config_keys.name: channel
config_keys.doc: ADC channel index (0..N). Defaults to 0.

peripherals.label: Photoresistor (LDR)
peripherals.summary: CdS photoresistor + voltage divider on an ADC channel.
peripherals.detail: Light-dependent-resistor model (R = 10 kΩ at 10 lx, gamma = 0.7) as the top leg of a 10 kΩ pull-down divider. Drive the `lux` channel at runtime and the ADC channel follows through the real resistance→voltage math: dark reads near 0 V, bright reads toward Vref. Starts at 100 lx (dim indoor light).

config_keys.name: channel
config_keys.doc: ADC channel index (0..N). Defaults to 0.

peripherals.label: MQ-6 Gas Sensor
peripherals.summary: Hanwei MQ-6 LPG/propane semiconductor gas sensor module (AOUT on ADC).
peripherals.detail: Module-level model of the common MQ-6 breakout: AOUT is a 0..Vref voltage that rises with LPG-equivalent concentration. Drive the `ppm` channel (0..10 000 ppm) at runtime and the ADC channel follows through a linear full-scale map. Starts at 0 ppm (clean air → ground rail). DOUT (comparator trip) is not modelled — compare AOUT in firmware.

config_keys.name: channel
config_keys.doc: ADC channel index (0..N). Defaults to 0.

peripherals.label: Soil Moisture Sensor
peripherals.summary: Capacitive soil moisture probe module (AOUT on ADC; wetter → lower voltage).
peripherals.detail: Module-level model of the common capacitive soil-moisture breakout: AOUT is a 0..Vref voltage that falls as the soil gets wetter. Drive the `moisture` channel (0..100 %) at runtime and the ADC channel follows. Starts at 40 % (slightly damp). DOUT (comparator trip) is not modelled — compare AOUT in firmware.

config_keys.name: channel
config_keys.doc: ADC channel index (0..N). Defaults to 0.

peripherals.label: 74HC595 Shift Register (8-bit output)
peripherals.summary: 8-bit serial-in / parallel-out shift register over SPI.
peripherals.detail: Lets a host MCU drive 8 outputs (QA..QH) through one SPI byte: SER→MOSI, SRCLK→SCK, RCLK→chip-select. Firmware shifts a byte then pulses RCLK to latch it onto the pins; the parallel outputs hold until that latch. Bit 7 = QH, bit 0 = QA (MSB-first).

config_keys.name: cs_pin
config_keys.doc: RCLK latch GPIO pin, wired as SPI chip-select (e.g. "PA4"). Defaults to PA4.

peripherals.label: VL53L1X ToF
peripherals.summary: Laser time-of-flight distance sensor over I2C.
peripherals.detail: STMicroelectronics VL53L1X at fixed address 0x29, modeled against the Pololu VL53L1X driver: MODEL_ID = 0xEACC, SYSTEM__MODE_START to begin ranging, GPIO__TIO_HV_STATUS data-ready poll, and the RESULT range burst. Reports a host-settable distance (default 500 mm); the ranging algorithm is not simulated.

config_keys.name: i2c_address
config_keys.doc: 7-bit slave address. Defaults to the VL53L1X fixed address 0x29.

peripherals.label: Sensirion SCD41 CO₂
peripherals.summary: Photoacoustic CO₂ + temperature + humidity sensor over I2C.
peripherals.detail: Sensirion SCD41 at fixed address 0x62, speaking the real Sensirion command protocol (16-bit commands, 16-bit words + CRC-8 poly 0x31), so the unmodified Sensirion SCD4x vendor driver decodes it on-target. The reported CO₂, temperature and humidity are externally driven inputs (channels co2 / temperature / humidity); config only seeds their initial values.

config_keys.name: i2c_address
config_keys.doc: 7-bit slave address. Defaults to the SCD41 fixed address 0x62.

config_keys.name: co2_ppm
config_keys.doc: Initial CO₂ the part reports, ppm. Default 450. Drive it at runtime with the `co2` input channel.

config_keys.name: temp_c
config_keys.doc: Initial temperature, °C. Default 22.0. Runtime channel: `temperature`.

config_keys.name: rh_pct
config_keys.doc: Initial relative humidity, %. Default 45.0. Runtime channel: `humidity`.

peripherals.label: Sensirion SGP41 VOC/NOx
peripherals.summary: MOx gas sensor returning raw VOC/NOx signals over I2C.
peripherals.detail: Sensirion SGP41 at fixed address 0x59, speaking the real Sensirion command protocol with CRC-8 (poly 0x31). Returns raw SRAW_VOC / SRAW_NOX ticks that the on-host Sensirion Gas Index Algorithm converts to a VOC Index. The raw signals are externally driven inputs (channels voc_sraw / nox_sraw); config only seeds their initial values.

config_keys.name: i2c_address
config_keys.doc: 7-bit slave address. Defaults to the SGP41 fixed address 0x59.

config_keys.name: voc_sraw
config_keys.doc: Initial raw VOC tick (~20000..40000). Default 28000. Drive it at runtime with the `voc_sraw` input channel.

config_keys.name: nox_sraw
config_keys.doc: Initial raw NOx tick. Default 16000. Runtime channel: `nox_sraw`.

peripherals.label: Sensirion SPS30 PM
peripherals.summary: Laser particulate-matter sensor (PM1/2.5/4/10) over I2C.
peripherals.detail: Sensirion SPS30 at fixed address 0x69, speaking the real Sensirion command protocol with CRC-8 (poly 0x31) in both float and uint16 output modes. The four mass concentrations are externally driven inputs (channels pm1_0 / pm2_5 / pm4_0 / pm10); config seeds them from one pm2_5 value. Drive it in uint16 mode on the ESP32-C3 (30-byte frame fits the controller's 32-byte FIFO).

config_keys.name: i2c_address
config_keys.doc: 7-bit slave address. Defaults to the SPS30 fixed address 0x69.

config_keys.name: pm2_5
config_keys.doc: Initial PM2.5 mass, µg/m³. Default 6.0 (clean indoor air). The other mass bins are seeded from it with the typical urban-aerosol ratios; each bin is drivable at runtime via its own input channel.

peripherals.label: Vishay VEML7700 Light
peripherals.summary: Ambient-light sensor (lux) over I2C.
peripherals.detail: Vishay VEML7700 at fixed address 0x10, a register-pointer device with 16-bit little-endian words. Reports a raw ALS count the firmware scales to lux at the default gain ×1 / 100 ms resolution (0.0576 lux/count). The illuminance is an externally driven input (channel lux); config only seeds its initial value.

config_keys.name: i2c_address
config_keys.doc: 7-bit slave address. Defaults to the VEML7700 fixed address 0x10.

config_keys.name: lux
config_keys.doc: Initial illuminance, lux. Default 450 (daylit room). Drive it at runtime with the `lux` input channel.

peripherals.label: Melexis MLX90614 IR Temp
peripherals.summary: Single-point IR (non-contact) thermometer over SMBus/I2C.
peripherals.detail: Melexis MLX90614 at address 0x5A. Reports an SMBus read-word (LSB, MSB, PEC) for ambient (0x06) and object/surface (0x07) temperature, encoded raw×0.02 K with a correct CRC-8 PEC. Both temperatures are externally driven inputs (channels surface_temp / ambient_temp); driving the surface below the air dew point is what makes firmware flag the surface condensation an air-only humidity index misses.

config_keys.name: i2c_address
config_keys.doc: 7-bit slave address. Defaults to the MLX90614 fixed address 0x5A.

config_keys.name: surface_temp_c
config_keys.doc: Initial surface (object) temperature, °C. Default 18. Drive it at runtime with the `surface_temp` input channel.

config_keys.name: ambient_temp_c
config_keys.doc: Initial ambient (chip) temperature reported on Ta, °C. Default 22. Runtime channel: `ambient_temp`.

peripherals.label: MAX7219 8×8 LED Matrix
peripherals.summary: 8×8 LED matrix driven by a MAX7219 serial display driver over SPI.
peripherals.detail: The common 8×8 dot-matrix module built around a MAX7219 common-cathode display driver. Firmware clocks 16 bits per transaction — an address byte then a data byte — and pulses CS/LOAD to latch. Digit registers 0x01–0x08 are the eight matrix rows, so digit RAM is the framebuffer; the model also tracks decode mode, intensity, scan limit, shutdown and display test, blanking or flooding the readback the way the real part drives the panel.

config_keys.name: cs_pin
config_keys.doc: CS/LOAD GPIO pin, wired as SPI chip-select (e.g. "PA4"). Defaults to PA4.

peripherals.label: LCD1602 Character Display (I2C)
peripherals.summary: 16×2 HD44780 character LCD behind a PCF8574 I2C backpack.
peripherals.detail: The ubiquitous blue/green 1602 module with an I2C adapter soldered on. Each I2C byte is a PCF8574 P-port snapshot (P7..P4 = D7..D4, P3 = backlight, P2 = E, P1 = RW, P0 = RS); the HD44780 latches a nibble on every falling edge of E and two latches make one instruction or character. The model tracks the full 80-byte DDRAM, the address counter, entry mode, display/cursor/blink flags and the backlight line, and the WASM bridge surfaces the visible 32 characters for the playground's display overlay.

config_keys.name: i2c_address
config_keys.doc: 7-bit slave address of the PCF8574 backpack. Defaults to 0x27 (PCF8574T); 0x3F is the other common strapping (PCF8574AT).
